program: v0.5 source review closure and capability decision preparation

risks.title: Local host is part of the trusted computing base
risks.risk: A compromised host, local admin, or Ithildin process can undermine local evidence and workspace controls.
risks.rationale: The product is a local mediation layer, not a kernel sandbox or endpoint security product.
mitigations: No shell, Docker socket, Kubernetes, browser automation, or broad filesystem write tools
risks.revisit_trigger: Before broader public/security-product positioning or any remote/production deployment claim

risks.title: Local principals are attribution labels
risks.risk: The local principal registry supports policy and review UX but is not proof of a real human, device, or enterprise identity.
risks.rationale: Production identity integrations are intentionally deferred.
mitigations: External review closure remains required before production identity claims
risks.revisit_trigger: Before OIDC/SAML/SCIM, remote MCP, multi-user deployments, or production authorization claims

risks.title: Audit is local tamper-evident evidence
risks.risk: SQLite, JSONL, and local signed exports do not provide immutable custody, external notarization, or independent retention.
risks.rationale: The current audit model is for local debugging, review, and evidence handoff.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before compliance, custody-grade audit, hosted evidence, or notarization claims

risks.title: Patch apply remains the only write path
risks.risk: Approval-gated stored patch apply is inherently higher risk than read tools because it mutates workspace files.
risks.rationale: The write surface is intentionally narrow and reviewable while broader writes stay deferred.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before adding any new write, delete, move, chmod, archive, shell, or plugin execution path

risks.title: Filesystem race claims are platform scoped
risks.risk: Path handling and race behavior are documented for macOS and Linux local-preview profiles only.
risks.rationale: Windows and WSL semantics require separate review before security support claims.
risks.closure_notes: GPT 5.5 Pro source-level review of the filesystem/platform bundle at commit 48b2a5f5ee3b173953780d03ae3985a0a64df104 closed EXT-FS-001 and found no new implementation findings for the v0.1 local-preview filesystem/platform lane.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before Windows/WSL support claims or broader filesystem mutation features

risks.title: HTTP fetch is narrow but SSRF-sensitive
risks.risk: URL normalization, DNS behavior, redirects, and private-network detection are subtle security boundaries.
risks.rationale: The tool is exact-allowlist, GET-only, and body-bounded, but still needs external/source review.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before arbitrary HTTP methods, headers, request bodies, cookies, broad network access, or remote deployment

risks.title: Policy engine remains local-preview scoped
risks.risk: YAML policy is canonical for local preview while OPA remains optional evidence/prototype support.
risks.rationale: Maintaining two policy paths without parity discipline could confuse decisions.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before making OPA canonical or adding policy editing/distribution features

risks.title: Signed evidence uses local operator keys
risks.risk: Local Ed25519 signatures prove local key possession, not external trust, hosted custody, or maintainer supply-chain signing.
risks.rationale: The trust root is intentionally local until a later evidence trust-root decision.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before release signing, external anchoring, key rotation policy, or notarization claims

risks.title: SQLite is the only runtime storage backend
risks.risk: SQLite is suitable for local preview but not multi-user hosted durability, retention, or concurrency guarantees.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before runtime Postgres, multi-user deployments, or production retention claims

risks.title: Redaction is best-effort leak reduction
risks.risk: Redaction can miss secrets and must not be treated as a guarantee that sensitive content cannot appear.
risks.rationale: The safest boundary is preventing exposure paths, with redaction as a secondary control.
risks.revisit_trigger: Before handling real secrets, secrets-manager tools, or production telemetry/export claims
